pin separation ⩾ 5.0 cm 1st set of lines present and in correct positions 1  and  = 50° 2° February/March 2025 Question Answer Marks justification matching statement (‘within limits of experimental accuracy’ / within 10% / close together owtte) 1 displacement between F and H less than in (b) 1 draw thin lines / use sharp pencil; look at base of pins / keep pins vertical; use thin pins; ensure pins are as far apart as possible February/March 2025 Question Answer Marks 4 MP1 circuit diagram: correct voltmeter circuit symbol shown in parallel with all or part of wire MP2 apparatus: metre ruler MP3 method: measure / record length; measure p.d. and current; specific reference to repeating with different length MP4 key / control variable: constant diameter / radius / guage of wire; material of wire / same wire / same type of wire MP5 table: columns, with units, for length, p.d., current, resistance MP6 analysis: compare readings in the table to see if change in length produces change in resistance; plot resistance-length or length-resistance graph MP7 additional point (one from): at least 5 sets of data taken; repeat for each length and take average; 2nd control variable: diameter / material / temperature of wire; use of protective resistor
